{"society_power": 3.0, "state_power": 4.0, "society_change": 0.0, "state_change": 0.0, "key_event": "Late Qing \"Self-Strengthening\" amid post-Taiping decentralization", "reasoning": "The late Qing state had a formal bureaucracy but its reach was weakened by the aftermath of the Taiping Rebellion, reliance on regional governors and their armies, unequal treaties, and limited fiscal capacity, placing it at the weak-to-moderate boundary. Civil society consisted of gentry networks, lineage organizations, guilds, and local associations that provided real but non-autonomous, locally bounded organization easily contained by the imperial order."}
